g.title: Panel surfaces (1640x560 = 2x 800x480 + 20px gutter + 60px header band + 20px footer band)
Variant headers above each 800x480 panel
g.text: Variant B — strict ADR-007, sidebar replacement + persistent strip (default, design to ship)
Variant A — pressure-test: slide-in panel + top banner + popover (not adopted unless SPIKE-001 promotes)

g.title: Variant B basemap (PMTiles surrogate). Map area x 0..480, y 78..516 (full height, no top banner). Sidebar at x 480..800. Bottom strip y 516..540.
7-node scenario: tag-1 normal, tag-2 SOS, tag-3 no-fix ghost, tag-4 stale, relay-1 healthy, relay-2 overdue, gw-0 gateway.

Always-visible map chrome: north arrow + N-lock, scale bar, zoom +/-, fit-all, home, clear-UI toggle, tile attribution. Bias rule #1 (always-visible chrome over popups). Per CLAUDE-DESIGN-PROMPT lines 85-91.

Variant B sidebar: detail view REPLACES the list per KIOSK-004 Variant B scope (tickets/KIOSK-004-selection-detail-panel.md). 320 px wide, full height. A ← Back-to-list row at top is the only chrome remaining. Bias rule #2 (sidebar replacement over slide-in).
g.text: RSSI / SNR / hop count — kiosk-lab data model

g.title: Persistent SOS state strip per KIOSK-006 Variant B (default). Strengthened visual hierarchy on the existing strip: - 24 px tall (was already 24 px; SOS state may grow to 28-32 px in impl) - DISTRESS in 13 pt bold white - Red fill Color32::from_rgb(160,28,28) per app.rs:245 - Right-aligned "read-only · ack at the tag" preserved per app.rs:275-278 No banner. No acknowledge button. No dismiss. The strip is the SOS surface.
g.text: · tag-2 · since 14:08:23 · flags.SOS=1 · last frame 42 s

g.title: Out-of-canvas inset showing how Variant B's bottom strip would carry the cursor coordinate readout WHILE TOUCH IS HELD, in a non-SOS state. Per KIOSK-002 Variant B (bottom-strip readout, no popover). Because this scenario is SOS-active, the live strip above is RED, and KIOSK-002 acceptance criterion 6 + KIOSK-002 ticket:80 (SOS > coord readout) prevents simultaneous display. That precedence is flagged as an UNRESOLVED design question, not pretended to be solved.

Variant A top-anchored SOS banner per KIOSK-006 Variant A. Sits above the map and pushes the map area down by 40 px (visible cost). Carries an ACKNOWLEDGE button which directly tests the ADR-007:46 prohibition on alert-acknowledgement flows. The PERSISTENT BOTTOM STRIP REMAINS UNDERNEATH per KIOSK-006:50 — the strip stays red whether or not the banner is dismissed. Variant A is a FAIR pressure-test: it ADDS a surface, it does not REMOVE the persistent state surface that Variant B keeps.
Variant A basemap. Same 7-node scenario as B. Map starts at y=100 because the banner above pushes it down by 22 px from B's y=78. The slide-in detail panel overlays the right portion of the map.

Same 7-node scene as Variant B. The slide-in panel covers the right ~280 px of the map; relay-1, tag-2's right edge, and tag-3 are partially or fully under the slide-in surface (a cost annotated at right).
Identical chrome to B. Buttons live in the still-visible left strip of the map and at the top-left because the slide-in covers the right.

Variant A coordinate readout per KIOSK-002 Variant A (popover near touch point). Pressure-test: the popover occludes the patch of map being read. Shown on the still-visible left strip of the map (the slide-in covers the right).

Variant A sidebar shows the LIST because the detail surface lives in the slide-in panel, not in the sidebar. STICKY ALERTS section pinned at top contains tag-2 (SOS) and tag-3 (NO FIX, SELECTED). Scrollable section contains tag-4, tag-1, relay-1, relay-2, gw-0. Row height 48 px per CLAUDE-DESIGN-PROMPT line 96.

Variant A slide-in detail panel per KIOSK-004 Variant A. ~280 px wide, overlays the right portion of the map area. Shows the same tag-3 detail as Variant B's sidebar replacement; the comparison is the SURFACE, not the content. Pressure-test: this panel covers the map (relay-1, tag-2 partially, tag-3 partially). The bottom strip and the banner remain visible.
g.text: RSSI / SNR / hop count — kiosk-lab data model

g.title: Variant A keeps the persistent SOS strip per KIOSK-006:50 — the strip stays red whether or not the banner is dismissed. Variant A ADDS the banner; it does not REMOVE the strip. (This is the fair pressure-test correction over the prior revision, which had Variant A revert the strip to calm. That made Variant A fail by giving up a required persistent state surface, which is not what KIOSK-006 Variant A actually proposes.)
g.text: · tag-2 · since 14:08:23 · flags.SOS=1 · last frame 42 s
read-only · ack at the tag (conflicts w/ banner button)

g.title: Marginal callouts. Every forbidden term ("acknowledge", "dashboard", "settings", "login", "CRUD", "dismiss") appears only with an exception marker on the same element per sarcom-svg-wireframe validator.
g.text: B · sidebar REPLACEMENT detail view (320 px, no overlay) — bias rule #2.
A · banner above map costs 22 px of vertical map area · ack button = ADR-007:46 tension.
A · sidebar shows LIST; detail moved to slide-in panel (covers map).

non-goal · neither variant renders: login screens, settings panes, CRUD forms, a remote dashboard, or any data entry (ADR-007:38-46; ARCHITECTURE.md:493-496). non-goal · no RSSI/SNR/hop fields (data model does not carry them). non-goal · no uncertainty disc on tag-3 (SPIKE-002 open). non-goal · no sentinel coordinates rendered as a marker (tag-3 ghost drawn at last_valid_fix_pos, not current sentinel pos).
